payload: {id:'N9BtFhRG4L',cs:50,ce:[[,,,,,,,,],[,,,,,,,,],[,,,,,,,,],[,,,,,,,,],[,,,,,,,,],[,,,,,,,,],[,,,,,,,,],[,,,,,,,,],[,,,,,,,,]],ca:[{v:'title: 3 Snakes 2 Puzzles'},{v:'author: the_cogito'},{v:'rules: Place each digit 1-9 exactly once in every row and column. Divide the grid into 3 snakes, which don\'t overlap and which collectively cover the entire grid. A snake is a 1-cell wide path of orthogonally connected cells with no branching. A snake may not touch itself, even diagonally. Cells separated by a black dot contain digits in a 2:1 ratio. Cells containing grey circles must contain odd digits. Thick borders between cells indicate those cells are a part of two different snakes. Adjacent cells along the same snake must contain digits that differ by exactly 4 or 5. '}],u:[{ct:[3.5,3.5],w:0.7,h:0.7,c1:CFCFCF,c2:CFCFCF,r:t},{ct:[8.5,8.5],w:0.7,h:0.7,c1:CFCFCF,c2:CFCFCF,r:t},{ct:[1,0.5],w:1,h:0.2,c1:#0,c2:#0,r:f},{ct:[8,8.5],w:1,h:0.2,c1:#0,c2:#0,r:f},{ct:[0.5,8],w:0.2,h:1,c1:#0,c2:#0,r:f},{ct:[8.5,1],w:0.2,h:1,c1:#0,c2:#0,r:f},{ct:[4.5,4],w:0.2,h:1,c1:#0,c2:#0,r:f}],o:[{ct:[0.5,1],w:0.3,h:0.3,c1:#0,c2:#0,r:t,fs:12,te:''}],vd:[{id:'P7WXcJVJRpE',publishedAt:'2022-06-25T19:30:02Z',t:'The Miracle Latin Square',d:0}]}
